result.summary: unified.to exposes a unified REST API, a remote MCP server, and a CLI; all developer surfaces use the same workspace API key, with regional API/MCP endpoints documented.
result.description: Unified.to provides a unified integration platform that lets applications connect to hundreds of third-party SaaS products through a single normalized API. It also offers embedded authorization and an MCP layer that exposes connected SaaS data and actions to LLM-based agents.

workspace_api_key.label: Workspace API key
workspace_api_key.setup: Sign in to the [Unified app dashboard](https://app.unified.to), then open [API settings](https://app.unified.to/settings/api) to view or create your workspace API token. The docs describe this token as your workspace API key/token and use it for both the REST API and MCP server. Keep it secret because it grants access to your workspace and, when used with a `connection` parameter in MCP, can access customer connection data.

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Choose the Unified MCP region; docs list `https://mcp-api.unified.to/mcp` and `https://mcp-api-eu.unified.to/mcp`.

variables.name: connection
variables.resolveFrom: For customer-data access, supply the Unified connection ID as the `connection` URL parameter; without it the server is workspace-only.
variables.description: End-customer connection ID for exposing tools against a specific Unified connection.

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Choose the Unified data region for your account; docs list North America `https://api.unified.to`, Europe `https://api-eu.unified.to`, and Australia `https://api-au.unified.to`.
